summary: The Goshen Township Board of Trustees held their regular meeting on March 12, 2024, covering routine business including bill payments, personnel matters, and community planning. The board approved bringing the Memorial Day parade back in-house, scheduled a cleanup day for June 1st, adopted a resolution requesting a speed study on Staggy Road, approved purchasing crash investigation equipment, appointed a new full-time police officer, and established a minimum checking account balance policy to maximize interest earnings in a money market account. Public comments addressed COVID-19 vaccine concerns and a congressional campaign.
decisions: Approved payment of bills for February 28 through March 12, 2024
Accepted resignation of part-time employee Connor moving to City of Cincinnati full-time
Clarified intent of resolution 004-2024 regarding marijuana businesses was to adopt and approve
Approved Memorial Day parade on Monday, May 27th at 10 a.m.
Approved Goshen cleanup day for Saturday, June 1st
Accepted resignation of Derek Holmes from the BZA effective 3-5-24
Adopted resolution 005-2024 requesting Clermont County Engineer and ODOT evaluate speed limit on Staggy Road
Approved purchase of MLID Reach RX network rover kit for crash investigations not to exceed $2,800
Adjourned into executive session at 6:31 p.m. under ORC 121.22G1
Appointed Gary Moen as full-time police officer contingent upon completion of pre-employment screening
Approved maintaining minimum balance of $2.25 million in LCNB primary checking account with remainder in CF Bank money market account
action_items: Donna Rosick to spearhead Memorial Day parade planning, coordinating with the Chamber
Administrator Rose and staff to plan Goshen cleanup day for June 1st
Chad (service department) to install speed limit signs on Staggy Road once study is complete
Chief Rhodes to complete pre-employment screening process for Gary Moen
Laura (fiscal officer) to transfer excess funds above $2.25 million from LCNB to CF Bank money market account
Clermont County Engineer and ODOT to conduct speed study on Staggy Road
key_amounts: $2,800 - not-to-exceed cost for MLID Reach RX network rover kit for crash investigations
$2.25 million - minimum balance to maintain in LCNB primary checking account
$1 million - initial deposit into CF Bank money market account on February 13
$1,957.79 - interest earned on $1 million in 16 days (approximately $4,000/month)
$200 - BZA filing fee discussed by public speaker Dave Math
$6,000 - original quote from Breathing Air to move SCBA compressor (done cheaper in-house)
$34.5 trillion - national debt referenced by congressional candidate Larry Kidd
notable_topics: Condolences for Phyllis Smith, wife of longtime road supervisor Bill Smith
COVID-19 vaccine concerns presented by Advocates for Medical Truth group
Congressional campaign speech by Larry Kidd
BZA filing fee refund concern raised by Dave Math regarding Tom Mackey case
Memorial Day parade brought back in-house from Chamber of Commerce
Speed limit evaluation for Staggy Road (currently unposted at 55 mph)
Officer Tyler Smith completed crash reconstructionist training
Hiring of Gary Moen as full-time police officer
Financial management - money market account strategy with CF Bank
SCBA compressor moved to Station 19 by service department saving significant cost
Medic unit delivery pushed back to July due to spring system back order
Part-time employee Connor leaving for City of Cincinnati full-time position
Executive session regarding public employee matters
attendees: Cahill Moola (public speaker - Advocates for Medical Truth)
notes: Meeting included an executive session from approximately 6:31 p.m. to 7:23 p.m. regarding personnel matters. The brick color decision referenced was apparently discussed previously and is moving forward. Trustee Hauserman mentioned an upcoming Board of Health (DAC) meeting and Clermont County Township Association meeting on the third Thursday of the month. The thank-you note read was from Melissa Witt regarding cemetery services for her mother Carol Van Landingham.
title: BOT 3-12-24
